channel.title: Aux Heat vs Emergency Heat on Utility Explained
channel.description: Recent content in Aux Heat vs Emergency Heat on Utility Explained

item.title: Aux Heat vs Emergency Heat: What They Mean on Your Thermostat (Plain English)
item.description:
The short answer

Aux Heat (auxiliary heat) usually means your heat pump is running backup heat to keep up with demand--often during cold snaps, defrost cycles, or when you raise the thermostat several degrees at once.

Emergency Heat is typically a manual mode you switch on when the heat pump itself can't run properly (for example, it's malfunctioning). In many homes, emergency heat uses the same backup heater as aux heat--but forces the heat pump to stay off.
